  "findings": [
    "Backstabbed push-in terminal used on a 20-ampere branch circuit",
    "Device listing limits push-in terminals to 15-ampere circuits and 14 AWG solid copper conductors"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-terminate the receptacle using the side-screw terminals (or back-wire clamp terminals if equipped)",
    "Verify conductor size and ensure proper torque is applied to the terminal screws per NEC 110.14(D)"
  ],
  "rationale": "Standard push-in (backstabbed) terminals on common receptacles are only listed and UL-approved for use with 14 AWG solid copper conductors on 15-ampere circuits. Using them on a 20-ampere circuit (which requires 12 AWG conductors) violates the manufacturer's listing instructions and NEC 110.3(B) / 110.14.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "UL 498"
  ]
}
